what_it_does: Implements a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server over stdio that exposes three read-only tools—search, read, and recall—for querying and retrieving indexed conversation and memory records.
observations: Defines Zod schemas for search and read inputs; the recall tool passes raw arguments through to the imported recall() function.
Registers three MCP tools: search (single or multi-concept), read (JSONL conversation display with optional line range), and recall (structured cross-session recall).
Search supports vector/text/both modes, date filters, project/session/source/git_branch filters, and markdown or JSON response formats.
All tools are annotated as read-only, idempotent, non-destructive, and closed-world.
Tool-level errors are returned as in-band text results with isError: true rather than as protocol errors.
Imports search, formatting, conversation display, recall, and version primitives from sibling source files.
standouts: The search tool accepts either a single semantic query string or an array of 2-5 concept strings for AND-style multi-concept search.
The recall tool explicitly advertises that it uses the same recall() core as the CLI and returns a stable RecallHit[] JSON contract.
Errors are deliberately surfaced inside tool results rather than thrown at the protocol layer.
conventions: Zod schemas validate MCP tool inputs and are mirrored by hand in the JSON Schema tool definitions.
Async request handlers use try/catch and return errors as in-band tool results.
Read-only tool annotations declare safety and idempotency for every exposed tool.
Shared core functions (search, recall, formatting) are imported rather than reimplemented.
concerns: The recall tool casts raw args to the expected types without a local Zod schema, so input validation happens inside recall() rather than at the MCP boundary.
The read tool uses synchronous fs.existsSync and fs.readFileSync inside an async handler.
There is no logging or tracing of individual tool invocations beyond the startup stderr message.
notes: This is the MCP adapter surface for the recollection system: it wires the existing search, read, and recall primitives into a standard MCP server over stdio.
questions: What is the exact shape and key set of the RecallHit[] contract returned by recall()?
Which embedding axes are valid and how are they registered?
What is the complete set of capture-source values supported beyond the examples given?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose—an MCP server providing search, read, and recall over indexed conversations—matches the implementation.
apparent_themes: Memory as a reusable service for agents.
Unified CLI and MCP surfaces sharing the same core functions.
Structured provenance filtering by session, source, project, branch, and date.
intention_signals: The file appears designed to make episodic memory accessible to any MCP client by wrapping existing search, read, and recall primitives in a standard protocol, emphasizing provenance scoping and stable JSON contracts.
novelty: The dual-mode search tool that accepts either a single semantic query or an array of concepts for AND search, and the explicit reuse of the same recall() core as the CLI.
contribution: Provides the MCP protocol binding and tool schema definitions that expose the recollection search, conversation reading, and structured recall capabilities to external agents.
summary_for_embedding: An MCP stdio server that registers search, read, and recall tools to let agents query and retrieve indexed conversation memory with filters and provenance.
intention_for_embedding: To offer a standard MCP interface over the recollection memory backend so agents can recover prior context before starting tasks.

symbols.does: Type alias inferred from the Zod SearchInputSchema, representing the validated input for the search tool.
symbols.description: Captures either a single query string or an array of 2-5 concept strings, plus mode, limit, date range, project, session, sources, git branch, and response format.

symbols.does: Type alias inferred from the Zod ShowConversationInputSchema, representing the validated input for the read tool.
symbols.description: Holds the absolute path to a JSONL conversation file and optional 1-indexed start/end line numbers for pagination.

symbols.does: Converts an unknown error value into a human-readable string prefixed with 'Error:'.
symbols.description: Used inside the tool call handler to turn exceptions into in-band text results rather than protocol-level errors.

symbols.does: Bootstraps the MCP server over stdio and connects it to the transport.
symbols.description: Logs a startup message to stderr, creates a StdioServerTransport, and awaits server.connect(transport); unhandled errors exit the process.
